channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Russia rehearses ‘use of nuclear forces’ in drills with Belarus
item.description: Russia delivered nuclear warheads to sites in Belarus as part of a three-day military exercise involving both countries.

item.title: Ecuador’s Disappeared
item.description: Fault Lines investigates forced disappearances conducted by Ecuador’s military while waging a war against drug cartels.

item.title: Arteta ‘built a fire’ instead of watching Arsenal’s Premier League crowning
item.description: Arsenal will lift the Premier League trophy for the first time since 2004 after Sunday's match at Crystal Palace.

item.title: US-Iran diplomacy picks up: What’s the latest?
item.description: Iranian media reported that the Pakistani interior minister is in Iran to help the US and Iran reach a peace deal.

item.title: French court finds Airbus, Air France guilty of manslaughter in 2009 crash
item.description: Airbus says it plans to appeal the ruling, which overturned 2023 acquittal of both companies.

item.title: Israel’s arrogance is becoming the evidence in the case against it
item.description: Ben-Gvir’s video of flotilla activists reveals a state so accustomed to impunity that it documents its own cruelty.

item.title: Germany make Neuer first-choice World Cup keeper to cap retirement U-turn
item.description: Manuel Neuer to start for Germany in World Cup on his return, despite Oliver ⁠Baumann long being labelled first choice.

item.title: The implosion of Keir Starmer’s Labour
item.description: The UK has had six prime ministers in 10 years. Is it ready for a seventh?

item.title: Sadio Mane, Kalidou Koulibaly star picks in Senegal’s World Cup squad
item.description: Iliman Ndiaye, Nicolas Jackson and Ismaila Sarr are other key players joining Mane in Senegal's World Cup forward line.

item.title: Activists take part in a land convoy from Libya to bring aid to Gaza
item.description: Activists take part in a land convoy from Libya to bring aid to Gaza

item.title: What’s behind the forced disappearances in Ecuador?
item.description: As Ecuador’s government cracks down on drug trafficking, how is it starting to impact civilians?

item.title: Maths behind Hormuz toll: Is paying Iran for transit cheaper than blockade?
item.description: Economics of Hormuz closure are skewed towards Iran, so why won't the vessels just pay Tehran and sail away?

item.title: What’s Trump’s ‘anti-weaponisation fund’ and why are legal experts alarmed?
item.description: Democrats argue that the fund will be used to compensate January 6 rioters pardoned by Trump.

item.title: India-Africa summit postponed as Ebola spreads to M23-held DR Congo area
item.description: Efforts to stop the latest outbreak of the deadly disease have been hampered by armed conflict in eastern DR Congo.

item.title: Neymar set to shake off injury scare to play for Brazil at World Cup
item.description: All-time record Brazil scorer Neymar misses Santos draw with San Lorenzo in the Copa Sudamericana due to calf injury.

item.title: Iran World Cup squad members apply for US, Canada visas in Turkiye
item.description: A number of players submitted visa applications in person at the US embassy in Ankara ahead of their training camp.

item.title: Mauritania’s female Islamic guides: Leading the fight against ‘extremism’
item.description: The fight against radicalisation in Mauritania advances through faith and community outreach by female Islamic guides.

item.title: How Ben-Gvir’s flotilla video shattered Israel’s multimillion ‘Hasbara’
item.description: The Israeli minister’s taunting of abducted activists triggers a global condemnation forcing Israel into damage control.

item.title: Israel begins deporting Gaza aid flotilla activists amid global outcry
item.description: Israel’s treatment of the hundreds of abductees has been condemned by several countries, including key allies.

item.title: This is the face of Israel
item.description: Ben Gvir’s video of bound flotilla activists showed Israel without the mask.

item.title: Gaza sisters win Earth Prize for turning war rubble into bricks
item.description: Palestinian sisters Tala and Farah Mousa turned the rubble of their bombed Gaza home into reusable bricks.

item.title: Outrage over Israel’s Ben-Gvir flotilla abuse video: What we know
item.description: In a video released by his office, National Security Minister Ben-Gvir taunts zip-tied activists, causing condemnation.

item.title: Canada is using its borders to police Palestine solidarity
item.description: Critics of Israel’s war on Gaza are increasingly facing interrogations, visa revocations and denials of entry.

item.title: How Chinese, Iranian companies profit in Russia-occupied Ukrainian regions
item.description: More than a dozen Chinese companies operate in Donetsk and Luhansk, according to a Ukrainian monitor.

item.title: Philippines orders arrest of fugitive senator wanted by ICC
item.description: Justice Secretary says anyone helping Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa evade arrest will 'face consequences'.
